title: agent-await-gates: design call on external-signal gates
body:
# Goal

Decide whether jjforge should ship beads-style external-
signal gates: a way for an issue to park itself on an
external condition (a GH PR landing, a timer expiring, a
human responding) instead of staying "open" forever.

This is a research ticket: pin the design before
implementing.

# Why now

beads (see `reference/beads/internal/types/types.go`
`await_type` / `await_id` fields, and statuses `deferred`
/ `hooked`) ships two related primitives:

- **`deferred` + `defer_until`**: time-based snooze.
  Hide from `bd ready` until the timer fires.
- **`hooked` + `await_type` + `await_id`**: external-
  signal gate. The issue's status is "waiting on X"
  where X is one of `gh:run`, `gh:pr`, `timer`, `human`,
  `mail`.

For jjforge, the use case is: a subagent finishes its
work and the next step depends on something external
(CI passes, a PR merges, a teammate confirms a decision).
Today the agent either: (a) leaves the issue open and
hopes someone notices, (b) writes a comment "blocked on
<thing>", (c) closes the issue prematurely. None of these
let `jjf ready` show the right thing at the right time.

But this is a meaningful design call. Polling for gh:pr
introduces a runtime dependency on GitHub's API. Polling
for `timer` is harmless but requires a daemon or a
cron-style poll-on-`jjf ready` decision. `human` requires
no polling but also doesn't auto-clear — someone has to
`jjf unblock <id>` it.

# Research questions

1. **Are await gates worth the complexity?** Or are they
   solved well enough by:
   - A `wait-on:` label as convention.
   - Closing the issue and re-filing on signal.
   - A simple `status: blocked` + a comment.

2. **Polling architecture.** If we do gates:
   - Pull model: `jjf ready` itself polls (slow if many
     gh:pr gates; auth required).
   - Push model: an external hook posts a comment that
     clears the gate when CI fires.
   - Manual model: an operator runs `jjf check-gates`
     periodically.

3. **Which gate types are worth shipping in v1?**
   Candidates:
   - `timer`: cheapest, most defensible. No external auth.
   - `human`: simple flag; no polling needed (someone
     `jjf clear <id>`s it).
   - `gh:pr`: useful but bring-your-own-auth. Forgejo
     (our remote) has different API.
   - `gh:run`: same caveat.
   - Forgejo equivalents: should we name-space (`fj:pr`?)
     or generalize (`pr:<host>:<owner>/<repo>/<num>`)?

4. **Spec impact.** Does this need a new status enum
   variant (`Awaiting`?) or can it be a flag on the
   existing `Status::Open`? beads chose the former;
   we'd need to weigh the spec-bump cost.

5. **Sequencing.** Should this land BEFORE or AFTER
   `agent-claim-atomic` (which also bumps the status
   enum)? If both, fold into one spec bump.

# Approach

Write a short design doc (2–3 pages) under
`docs/agent-await-gates-design.md` that:

- Restates the use case with two concrete jjforge
  scenarios (one for `timer`, one for `gh:pr`).
- Compares against the three "good enough" alternatives.
- Picks a polling architecture (or argues for none).
- Names the v1 gate types.
- Names the v1 CLI: `jjf await <id> --until <spec>` /
  `jjf clear <id>` / `jjf check-gates`.
- Decides spec impact (new status variant vs flag).
- Lands a recommendation: "ship it as ticket X" or
  "skip it; use the label-convention escape hatch."

# Acceptance

- [ ] `docs/agent-await-gates-design.md` exists with the
  sections above filled in.
- [ ] Recommendation pinned in closing comment.
- [ ] If recommendation is "ship it," a follow-up ticket
  is filed with the concrete v1 scope.
- [ ] If recommendation is "skip it," the closing comment
  documents why so future sessions don't re-open the
  question without new evidence.

# Out of scope

- Implementation. This is research → decision → optional
  follow-up ticket.
- Forgejo API integration. Even if gates ship, that's its
  own implementation ticket.
- A daemon. v1 is "polled by `jjf ready` or `jjf check-gates`"
  if it ships at all.

# Pointers

- Reference: `reference/beads/internal/types/types.go`
  (`await_type` / `await_id` / status enum) and
  `reference/beads/cmd/bd/await.go` if present.
- jjforge status enum: `crates/jjf-storage/src/record.rs`.
- Pull vs push architectures discussed in
  `reference/beads/docs/ARCHITECTURE.md`.
